title: ExoVeil: Detecting Exoplanets by Single Transit via Self-Supervision
oneliner: The ExoVeil system learns to predict stellar brightness and finds single transits that classifiers miss.
description: The ExoVeil system is presented, using a transformer world model to predict stellar brightness and detect transits from residuals. The system does not require phase folding and can find planets with a single transit. It demonstrates recovery of 47 confirmed TESS planets without retraining and sensit

title: Neutrino Fingerprints: Images for Direction Reconstruction in IceCube
oneliner: Encoding IceCube detector data into compact RGB images allows a convolutional neural network to achieve neutrino direction reconstruction accuracy close to complex ensemble models.
description: Researchers developed a method to encode events from the IceCube neutrino observatory into 72×72 pixel images (neutrino fingerprints). Using a standard ResNet18 network, they achieved a mean angular error of 1.10 rad, only 0.15 rad worse than top Kaggle solutions based on graph neural networks and t

title: First Detection of HC5N in the TW Hya Disk: Long Carbon Chains in the Cradle of Planets
oneliner: Astronomers have for the first time detected the HC5N molecule in a protoplanetary disk, showing that complex carbon chemistry is possible even in cold planet-forming regions.
description: Using the ALMA radio telescope, scientists recorded the faint emission of the cyanopolyyne molecule HC5N in the disk around the star TW Hya. This is the longest carbon chain found in a Class II disk, and it provides a key to understanding prebiotic chemistry in planetary systems.

title: Nereid: The Last Surviving Regular Moon of Neptune
oneliner: New JWST data and dynamical modeling show that Nereid is not a captured Kuiper Belt object but the sole surviving regular moon of Neptune.
description: Using JWST spectroscopy and N-body simulations, the authors propose a new origin for Nereid—Neptune's third-largest moon. Its composition and orbit do not match captured irregular moons but rather point to in-situ formation and subsequent perturbation by Triton.

title: High-Performance Combining Panel for Broadband Radio Interferometers
oneliner: A new combining panel architecture delivers stable synchronization and high throughput for the next generation of radio interferometers.
description: A combining panel has been developed for the CRS platform, supporting up to 128 digitized inputs per crate at 25 Gbps per line. The system demonstrates sub-picosecond delay stability and jitter of a few hundred femtoseconds, making it ideal for demanding applications such as the CHORD telescope.

title: Neutron Star Mass in 4U 1820–30: A Fresh Perspective
oneliner: Analysis of gravitational redshift, pulsations, and bursts reveals contradictions in neutron star mass estimates.
description: The article reexamines the mass of the neutron star in the ultracompact binary system 4U 1820–30, considering the recently discovered absorption line at 3.8 keV, interpreted as a gravitationally redshifted iron line. It shows that the derived redshift of 1+z=1.72 implies high compactness, which is d

title: Cosmic Birefringence: Hybrid ILC Breaks the Degeneracy
oneliner: A new hybrid ILC method measures cosmic birefringence by separating it from instrumental effects, yielding a 2.7σ hint of its existence in Planck data.
description: Cosmic birefringence — the rotation of the polarization plane of the cosmic microwave background, predicted by physics beyond the Standard Model. Its measurement is complicated by degeneracy with instrumental calibration. This work presents a hybrid ILC method that exploits the achromatic nature of

title: SeismoGPT: Predicting Earthquakes with AI to Protect Gravitational Wave Observatories
oneliner: The neural network SeismoGPT, based on transformers, learned to predict seismic waves, which could help future gravitational wave detectors.
description: SeismoGPT is a 26-million parameter causal transformer that predicts three-component seismograms. The model is trained on synthetic data and achieves a median NCC above 0.93. Predictions worsen at large distances and small magnitudes. This work paves the way for using AI in seismic warning and noise

title: Neutron Star Cooling: A New Explanation Without 'Surface Heating'
oneliner: A new boundary condition accounting for thermonuclear burning of hydrogen and helium explains neutron star cooling after accretion outbursts without invoking an unknown heat source.
description: Astrophysicists simulated neutron star cooling after accretion episodes and found that a new boundary condition accounting for thermonuclear hydrogen and helium burning in the envelope reproduces observations without the need for ad hoc 'surface heating.'
